map.name: F_Pinchuk_Campbell_padded
map.construction: Pinchuk 1994 real-plane counterexample to the strong real Jacobian conjecture, in the Campbell/van den Essen explicit form (arXiv:1001.3318): polynomial F=(P,Q):R^2->R^2 of degrees (10,25), padded by idle z. det DF = t^2+(t+f(13+15h))^2+f^2 > 0 everywhere (sum of squares; t=f=0 impossible over R). Not injective (explicit collisions certified by CAS). Not surjective (misses exactly two points: (0,0) and (-1,-163/4) per Campbell). Dual field X1 = H(P)/j = (-P_y, P_x)/j satisfies DF·X1=e1 and dQ/dt=1. On every level P=c with c>-1 the real level curve has poles h=-1±sqrt(1+c) at which (x,y)->infinity while Q approaches a finite asymptotic value: X1 incomplete in finite time. The incompleteness locus therefore contains the open set {P>-1} (positive measure) — OPEN-SET incompleteness for a genuinely polynomial 2D Keller-type map, contrasting A007's thin/null-set incompleteness (non-polynomial).

jacobian.det_constant: t^2 + (t + f*(13+15*h))^2 + f^2  (not constant; nowhere zero / certified sum-of-squares)

placeholders.poisson_lift_Phi: B = J^{-T}; J polynomial, det nowhere zero so B rational/smooth
placeholders.star_domain_status: X1 incomplete on open set {P>-1}; X0 incomplete at least on level sets through missing values (thin forced set) and possibly more; ESS/deficiency not computed

literature: S. Pinchuk, A counterexample to the strong real Jacobian conjecture, Math. Z. 217 (1994), 1-4
L.A. Campbell, The asymptotic variety of a Pinchuk map as a polynomial curve, arXiv:1001.3318
L.A. Campbell, Picturing Pinchuk's plane polynomial pair, arXiv:math/9812032
A. van den Essen, Polynomial automorphisms and the Jacobian conjecture, PM 190, Birkhauser, 2000

composition_notes: B001 v0.4 dichotomy evidence: first genuinely 2D polynomial non-injective Keller-type atlas row with OPEN incompleteness locus. Supports (does not prove) polynomial=>open-only dichotomy vs A007 thin smooth failure. det>0 not constant: counterexample to strong real JC, not to complex JC(2).
non_claims: Not a complex Jacobian conjecture counterexample (real plane; det not constant)
No general polynomial-Keller dichotomy theorem (single example is evidence)

evidence: det DF = sum of squares > 0; collisions CAS-certified; X1 incomplete on open set {P>-1} via level-set pole / finite Delta-Q argument (Campbell asymptotic variety)
